---
title: "Package Tools"
---

`ir tool run` resolves a package and runs an executable from that package's `exec/` directory.

```console
$ ir tool run --from btw btw --help
$ ir tool run btw --help
$ ir tool run --from github::r-lib/Rapp Rapp
```

For `ir tool run --from pkg tool`, `ir` installs `pkg`, finds `exec/tool` or `exec/tool.R`, and launches it with the selected Rscript. A bare self-named package ref such as `ir tool run btw` is shorthand for `ir tool run --from btw btw`.

Tool runs support the same dependency and R selection flags as script runs:

```console
$ ir tool run --with cli --from btw btw
$ ir tool run --from 'btw>=0.1.0' btw
$ ir tool run --r-version 4.5 --from btw btw
```

Package refs use pak syntax. Quote refs when the shell would otherwise interpret characters such as `>`.

## Install launchers

`ir tool install` resolves a package, scans that package's `exec/` directory, and writes launchers for the executables it finds.

```console
$ ir tool install btw
$ ir tool install github::r-lib/Rapp
$ ir tool install --bin-dir ~/.local/bin btw
$ ir tool install --force btw
```

Installed launchers keep the same resolved package ref, dependency additions, and R version selection that were used at install time.
